datasets.name: ds_trip_reliability
datasets.query:
SELECT
  service_date,
  route_id,
  direction_id,
  COUNT(*)                                             AS total_trips,
  SUM(CASE WHEN data_quality_flag IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS trips_with_data,
  ROUND(
    SUM(CASE WHEN data_quality_flag IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1
  )                                                    AS data_coverage_pct,
  -- FTA OTP: -60s early to +299s late
  ROUND(AVG(
    CASE
      WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds BETWEEN -60 AND 299 THEN 1.0
      WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds IS NOT NULL THEN 0.0
    END
  ) * 100, 1)                                          AS otp_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(total_trip_delay_seconds) / 60.0, 2)       AS avg_delay_min,
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_APPROX(total_trip_delay_seconds, 0.9) / 60.0, 2) AS p90_delay_min,
  ROUND(AVG(late_start_seconds) / 60.0, 2)             AS avg_late_start_min,
  ROUND(
    PERCENTILE_APPROX(
      UNIX_TIMESTAMP(actual_end_ts) - UNIX_TIMESTAMP(actual_start_ts), 0.5
    ) / 60.0, 1
  )                                                    AS p50_runtime_min
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_trip_timeline_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
GROUP BY service_date, route_id, direction_id
ORDER BY service_date DESC, otp_pct ASC

datasets.name: ds_trip_delay_dist
datasets.query:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds < -60   THEN '< -1 min (very early)'
    WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds < 0     THEN '-1–0 min (slightly early)'
    WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds < 60    THEN '0–1 min (on time)'
    WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds < 180   THEN '1–3 min (negligible late)'
    WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds < 360   THEN '3–6 min (moderate late)'
    ELSE '6+ min (severe late)'
  END                                                  AS delay_bucket,
  COUNT(*)                                             AS trip_count,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1)  AS pct_of_total
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_trip_timeline_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND data_quality_flag IS NULL
  AND total_trip_delay_seconds IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY delay_bucket
ORDER BY MIN(total_trip_delay_seconds)

datasets.name: ds_late_start_analysis
datasets.query:
SELECT
  route_id,
  direction_id,
  COUNT(*)                                             AS trips,
  ROUND(AVG(late_start_seconds) / 60.0, 2)             AS avg_late_start_min,
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_APPROX(late_start_seconds, 0.9) / 60.0, 2) AS p90_late_start_min,
  SUM(CASE WHEN late_start_seconds > 180 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS trips_late_start_3min_plus,
  ROUND(
    SUM(CASE WHEN late_start_seconds > 180 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1
  )                                                    AS pct_late_start_3min_plus
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_trip_timeline_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND data_quality_flag IS NULL
  AND late_start_seconds IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY route_id, direction_id
HAVING trips >= 5
ORDER BY avg_late_start_min DESC
LIMIT 50

datasets.name: ds_system_daily_kpi
datasets.query:
SELECT
  service_date,
  COUNT(*)                                             AS total_trips,
  SUM(CASE WHEN data_quality_flag IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS trips_with_data,
  ROUND(
    SUM(CASE WHEN data_quality_flag IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1
  )                                                    AS data_coverage_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(
    CASE
      WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds BETWEEN -60 AND 299 THEN 1.0
      WHEN total_trip_delay_seconds IS NOT NULL THEN 0.0
    END
  ) * 100, 1)                                          AS system_otp_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN data_quality_flag IS NULL THEN total_trip_delay_seconds END) / 60.0, 2) AS system_avg_delay_min,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN data_quality_flag IS NULL THEN late_start_seconds END) / 60.0, 2) AS system_avg_late_start_min
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_trip_timeline_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
GROUP BY service_date
ORDER BY service_date

datasets.name: ds_severe_trips
datasets.displayName: Severely Late Trips (≥6 min, Last 7 Days)
datasets.query:
SELECT
  service_date,
  trip_id,
  route_id,
  direction_id,
  ROUND(total_trip_delay_seconds / 60.0, 1)            AS delay_min,
  ROUND(late_start_seconds / 60.0, 1)                  AS late_start_min,
  stop_count,
  ROUND(
    (UNIX_TIMESTAMP(actual_end_ts) - UNIX_TIMESTAMP(actual_start_ts)) / 60.0, 1
  )                                                    AS actual_runtime_min,
  ROUND(
    (UNIX_TIMESTAMP(planned_end_ts) - UNIX_TIMESTAMP(planned_start_ts)) / 60.0, 1
  )                                                    AS scheduled_runtime_min
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_trip_timeline_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND total_trip_delay_seconds >= 360
  AND data_quality_flag IS NULL
ORDER BY service_date DESC, delay_min DESC
LIMIT 200

widget.name: txt_kpi_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## System Trip Health KPIs
Delay bands (CAD/AVL): <1 min=negligible, 1–3 min=moderate, 3–6 min=significant, ≥6 min=severe. OTP window: -60s early to +299s late (FTA standard).

widget.name: txt_late_start_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## Late Start Analysis
Late start = actual departure at first stop minus scheduled departure. Persistent late starts indicate scheduling issues or terminal congestion.

viz_spec.title: Late Start by Route — Last 7 Days

widget.name: txt_severe_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## Severely Late Trips (≥6 min)
These are the worst performers from the last 7 days. Use trip_id to cross-reference with the playback path in `gold_trip_path_fact`.

viz_spec.title: Severely Late Trips — Last 7 Days (≥6 min delay)

widget.name: txt_route_rel_header
widget.textbox_spec:
## Route Reliability — Last 30 Days
Grain: (service_date, route_id, direction_id). Data coverage = trips with actual GTFS-RT data vs total scheduled.
